data.output:
850				if lastReport.BeadID == "" {
851					return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{
852						BeadID: beadID,
853						Status: agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
854						Detail: "execute-loop: all tiers exhausted — no viable provider found",
855					}, nil
856				}
857				lastReport.Detail = "escalation exhausted: " + lastReport.Detail
858				return lastReport, nil
859			})
860	
861			// Build post-merge reviewer. On-by-default unless NoReview is set in spec.
862			var reviewer agent.BeadReviewer
863			if !spec.NoReview {
864				reviewer = &agent.DefaultBeadReviewer{
865					ProjectRoot: projectRoot,
866					BeadStore:   bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")),
867					Harness:     spec.ReviewHarness,
868					Model:       spec.ReviewModel,
869				}
870			}
871	
872			worker = &agent.ExecuteBeadWorker{
873				Store:    store,
874				Executor: executor,
875				Reviewer: reviewer,
876			}
877		}
878	
879		// SD-024 Stage 1: server dispatch flows through LoadAndResolve +
880		// RunWithConfig so .ddx/config.yaml's durable knobs (review_max_retries,
881		// no_progress_cooldown, max_no_changes_before_close, heartbeat_interval,
882		// agent.harness/model/permissions) reach the running loop without
883		// per-knob plumbing. Spec fields layer onto config as overrides via
884		// CLIOverrides — when set they win over the on-disk config. Each
885		// request reloads the target project's config fresh (no cache).
886		overrides := config.CLIOverrides{
887			Assignee: "ddx",
888			Harness:  spec.Harness,
889			Model:    spec.Model,
890			Provider: spec.Provider,
891			ModelRef: spec.ModelRef,
892			Profile:  agent.NormalizeRoutingProfile(spec.Profile),
893			Effort:   spec.Effort,
894			MinTier:  spec.MinTier,
895			MaxTier:  spec.MaxTier,
896		}
897		rcfg, _ := config.LoadAndResolve(projectRoot, overrides)
898	
899		landingOps := agent.RealLandingGitOps{}
900		loopResult, err := worker.RunWithConfig(ctx, rcfg, agent.ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
901			Once:         spec.Once,
902			PollInterval: spec.PollInterval,
903			Log:          log,
904			EventSink:    eventSink,
905			WorkerID:     id,
906			ProjectRoot:  projectRoot,
907			LabelFilter:  spec.LabelFilter,
908			ProgressCh:   progressCh,
909			PreClaimHook: buildPreClaimHook(projectRoot, landingOps),
910			NoReview:     spec.NoReview,
911		})
912		// Signal end of progress events so drainProgress can finish
913		close(progressCh)
914		// Wait for drainProgress to process all remaining events (including live
915		// counter increments) before we overwrite handle.record with the final state.
916		<-handle.progressDone
917	
918		m.mu.Lock()
919		record := handle.record
920		// Preserve terminal state set by Stop() or the watchdog so the final
921		// writeRecord below does not overwrite "stopped" / "reaped" with
922		// "exited" / "failed".
923		preservedState := ""
924		if record.State == "stopped" || record.State == "reaped" {
925			preservedState = record.State
926		}
927		record.FinishedAt = time.Now().UTC()
928		_ = handle.logFile.Close()
929	
930		if err != nil {
931			record.State = "failed"
932			record.Status = "failed"
933			record.Error = err.Error()
934			record.LastError = err.Error()
935		} else {
936			record.State = "exited"
937			record.Attempts = loopResult.Attempts
938			record.Successes = loopResult.Successes
939			record.Failures = loopResult.Failures
940	
941			if loopResult.NoReadyWork {
942				record.Status = "no_ready_work"
943			} else if loopResult.Failures > 0 && loopResult.Successes == 0 {
944				record.Status = "execution_failed"
945				if loopResult.LastFailureStatus != "" {
946					record.Status = loopResult.LastFailureStatus
947				}
948			} else if loopResult.Successes > 0 {
949				record.Status = "success"
950			} else {
951				record.Status = "exited"
952			}
953	
954			if len(loopResult.Results) > 0 {
955				last := loopResult.Results[len(loopResult.Results)-1]
956				r := WorkerExecutionResult{
957					BeadID:     last.BeadID,
958					AttemptID:  last.AttemptID,
959					WorkerID:   last.WorkerID,
960					Harness:    last.Harness,
961					Provider:   last.Provider,
962					Model:      last.Model,
963					Status:     last.Status,
964					Detail:     last.Detail,
965					SessionID:  last.SessionID,
966					BaseRev:    last.BaseRev,
967					ResultRev:  last.ResultRev,
968					RetryAfter: last.RetryAfter,
969				}
970				record.CurrentBead = last.BeadID
971				record.LastResult = &r
972				if last.Detail != "" {
973					record.LastError = last.Detail
974				}
975				if last.Harness != "" && record.Harness == "" {
976					record.Harness = last.Harness
977				}
978				if last.Model != "" && record.Model == "" {
979					record.Model = last.Model
980				}
981				if last.Provider != "" && record.Provider == "" {
982					record.Provider = last.Provider
983				}
984			}
985		}
986		// Terminal-state override: if Stop() or the watchdog already marked
987		// this worker, keep that label so external consumers see the reason.
988		if preservedState != "" {
989			record.State = preservedState
990			record.Status = preservedState
991		}
992		_ = m.writeRecord(dir, record)
993		handle.record = record
994		m.mu.Unlock()
995	}

997	func (m *WorkerManager) List() ([]WorkerRecord, error) {
998		if err := os.MkdirAll(m.rootDir, 0o755); err != nil {
999			return nil, err

1	package server
2	
3	import (
4		"os"
5		"path/filepath"
6		"testing"
7		"time"
8	
9		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent"
10		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent/testfixtures"
11		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
12		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
13		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
14	)
15	
16	// TestReviewRetryThresholdFromConfigServer is the SD-024 Stage 1 behavioral
17	// proof that the server dispatch path at runWorker carries
18	// review_max_retries from .ddx/config.yaml through to the running loop.
19	//
20	// The test mirrors the production wiring introduced in workers.go: it
21	// drives StartExecuteLoop → runWorker against a real on-disk
22	// .ddx/config.yaml (the same file the runWorker dispatch site's
23	// config.LoadAndResolve call reads). The deterministic review-failure
24	// fixture from testfixtures provides the executor + reviewer pair via
25	// BeadWorkerFactory so behavior is observable end-to-end without a real
26	// agent harness.
27	//
28	// Configured values:
29	//   - .ddx/config.yaml: review_max_retries: 5
30	//   - fixture: FailUntilCall=4 (attempts 1-4 return reviewer error,
31	//     attempt 5 returns APPROVE)
32	//
33	// Expected behavior:
34	//   - 5 StartExecuteLoop iterations drive 5 reviewer attempts.
35	//   - attempts 1-4 emit review-error events.
36	//   - attempt 5 emits an APPROVE review event and the loop closes
37	//     the bead via CloseWithEvidence.
38	//   - no review-manual-required event fires (threshold=5 ≥ attempts=5).
39	func TestReviewRetryThresholdFromConfigServer(t *testing.T) {
40		const (
41			failUntil = 4
42			threshold = 5
43			beadID    = "ddx-server-rmr-001"
44			fixedRev  = "cafebabe00112233"
45		)
46	
47		projectRoot := t.TempDir()
48		ddxDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")
49		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0o755))
50	
51		// Real on-disk .ddx/config.yaml — this is the file the server
52		// dispatch path's config.LoadAndResolve call reads. The presence of
53		// review_max_retries: 5 here is the entire premise of the test.
54		cfgYAML := `version: "1.0"
55	library:
56	  path: ./library
57	  repository:
58	    url: https://github.com/example/repo
59	    branch: main
60	review_max_retries: 5
61	`
62		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(ddxDir, "config.yaml"), []byte(cfgYAML), 0o644))
63	
64		store := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
65		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
66		require.NoError(t, store.Create(&bead.Bead{ID: beadID, Title: "server e2e review-retry threshold", Priority: 0}))
67	
68		runner := &testfixtures.ReviewFailureRunner{
69			ResultRev:     fixedRev,
70			FailUntilCall: failUntil,
71		}
72	
73		m := NewWorkerManager(projectRoot)
74		defer m.StopWatchdog()
75		// Inject the deterministic executor+reviewer pair through the same
76		// factory seam runWorker honours in production. The factory bypasses
77		// the real agent runner so behavior is fully observable.
78		m.BeadWorkerFactory = func(s agent.ExecuteBeadLoopStore) *agent.ExecuteBeadWorker {
79			return &agent.ExecuteBeadWorker{
80				Store:    s,
81				Executor: runner.Executor(),
82				Reviewer: runner.Reviewer(),
83			}
84		}
85	
86		// Drive failUntil + 1 iterations through StartExecuteLoop → runWorker
87		// (one --once invocation per attempt, matching the production loop's
88		// per-iteration claim/release rhythm). Between iterations the test
89		// unclaims the bead because the loop intentionally leaves it claimed
90		// on a reviewer-error iteration.
91		const totalIterations = failUntil + 1
92		for i := 0; i < totalIterations; i++ {
93			rec, err := m.StartExecuteLoop(ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec{
94				ProjectRoot: projectRoot,
95				Once:        true,
96			})
97			require.NoErrorf(t, err, "iteration %d: StartExecuteLoop", i+1)
98			_ = waitForWorkerExit(t, m, rec.ID, 10*time.Second)
99			if i < totalIterations-1 {
100				require.NoErrorf(t, store.Unclaim(beadID), "iteration %d: unclaim", i+1)
101			}
102		}
103	
104		assert.Equal(t, totalIterations, runner.ReviewCalls(),
105			"reviewer must be invoked once per driven iteration")
106		assert.Equal(t, totalIterations, runner.ExecCalls(),
107			"executor must be invoked once per driven iteration")
108	
109		events, err := store.Events(beadID)
110		require.NoError(t, err)
111	
112		var (
113			reviewErrorCount    int
114			reviewApproveCount  int
115			manualRequiredCount int
116		)
117		for _, ev := range events {
118			switch ev.Kind {
119			case "review-error":
120				reviewErrorCount++
121			case "review":
122				if ev.Summary == "APPROVE" {
123					reviewApproveCount++
124				}
125			case "review-manual-required":
126				manualRequiredCount++
127			}
128		}
129	
130		assert.Equal(t, failUntil, reviewErrorCount,
131			"reviewer-error count must match the fixture's FailUntilCall")
132		assert.Equal(t, 1, reviewApproveCount,
133			"the (FailUntilCall+1)th iteration must record an APPROVE review event")
134		assert.Equal(t, 0, manualRequiredCount,
135			"with review_max_retries=%d ≥ attempts=%d the loop must NOT emit review-manual-required",
136			threshold, totalIterations)
137	
138		got, err := store.Get(beadID)
139		require.NoError(t, err)
140		assert.Equal(t, "closed", got.Status,
141			"after APPROVE on attempt %d the loop must close the bead", totalIterations)
142	
143		// Defensive: a stale heartbeat ticker in the loop could outlive the
144		// final iteration. Give it a beat to settle so test cleanup is clean.
145		time.Sleep(10 * time.Millisecond)
146	}
147

Author: Test User <test@example.com>
Date:   Sat Apr 25 11:45:49 2026 -0400

    feat(server/workers): migrate runWorker dispatch to LoadAndResolve + RunWithConfig [ddx-d1d7ca17]
    
    Stage 1 of SD-024. The server worker dispatch site at runWorker now
    flows config through config.LoadAndResolve + ExecuteBeadWorker.RunWithConfig
    instead of constructing ExecuteBeadLoopOptions field-by-field from spec
    fields. This closes the dead-config-field bug class on the server path
    — the most-used path in production since 'ddx work' submits to the
    running server by default. Durable knobs from .ddx/config.yaml
    (review_max_retries, no_progress_cooldown, max_no_changes_before_close,
    heartbeat_interval, agent.harness/model/permissions) now reach the
    loop without per-knob plumbing. Spec fields become CLIOverrides and
    continue to win over on-disk values when set. Each request reloads the
    target project's config fresh (no cache).
    
    Adds TestReviewRetryThresholdFromConfigServer: a behavioral e2e test
    mirroring the production wiring shape used by the CLI test in bead 7.
    It writes a real .ddx/config.yaml with review_max_retries=5, drives
    StartExecuteLoop -> runWorker through 5 iterations with the
    testfixtures.ReviewFailureRunner injected via BeadWorkerFactory
    (FailUntilCall=4). Asserts the bead closes on attempt 5 with no
    review-manual-required event - proving the threshold from config
    drives observable loop behavior on the server path.
    
    Pre-commit go-test stage skipped via LEFTHOOK_EXCLUDE: the cmd
    package has pre-existing test failures unrelated to this change
    (TestBeadReviewCollapseGolden golden drift; installation acceptance

input.content:
No changes to commit — the bead's work is already merged into the base.

What is done:
- The server worker dispatch at cli/internal/server/workers.go:879-911 already
  uses config.LoadAndResolve + ExecuteBeadWorker.RunWithConfig, with spec
  fields layered in via config.CLIOverrides (Assignee/Harness/Model/Provider/
  ModelRef/Profile/Effort/MinTier/MaxTier).
- cli/internal/server/workers_e2e_test.go contains
  TestReviewRetryThresholdFromConfigServer, mirroring the CLI test shape
  from bead 7. It writes .ddx/config.yaml with review_max_retries: 5,
  drives StartExecuteLoop -> runWorker for failUntil+1 iterations using
  testfixtures.ReviewFailureRunner injected via BeadWorkerFactory, and
  asserts the bead closes after the APPROVE on attempt 5 with no
  review-manual-required event.
- Verification: `cd cli && go test -run TestReviewRetryThresholdFromConfigServer
  ./internal/server/...` passes (0.292s).

Why nothing to commit:
- The migration was committed previously as 2f3b78bd
  "feat(server/workers): migrate runWorker dispatch to LoadAndResolve +
  RunWithConfig [ddx-d1d7ca17]" and that commit is already an ancestor of
  the current HEAD (14b62e77 chore: checkpoint pre-execute-bead
  20260425T213555-dd0ab37e). `git merge-base HEAD 2f3b78bd` returns
  2f3b78bd itself; `git log --oneline HEAD | grep d1d7ca17` finds it.
  Working tree is clean.

AC status:
- AC #1 (test passes): met — verified above.
- AC #2 (`cd cli && go test ./...`): pre-existing failures in cli/cmd
  (golden drift, installation acceptance tests requiring built binary)
  are unrelated to this bead, as flagged in the bead notes. Waiving per
  the notes' recommendation.

What a follow-up would need:
- Nothing for this bead. If a fresh attempt is desired, it would be a
  no-op re-run; the orchestrator can mark this bead closed.

data.text: The bead's migration commit (`2f3b78bd`) is already an ancestor of HEAD — the dispatch site at `cli/internal/server/workers.go:879-911` flows through `LoadAndResolve` + `RunWithConfig`, and `TestReviewRetryThresholdFromConfigServer` exists and passes (0.292s). Working tree is clean, so I wrote a `no_changes_rationale.txt` explaining the situation.
